item.title: Merck PD-1/VEGF Bispecific (MK-1308A)
item.description:
• Summary: Merck presented early clinical data for its bispecific antibody MK-1308A, targeting PD-1 and VEGF, in non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) at AACR. The data showed promising antitumor activity, though phase 3 plans remain undisclosed. This agent combines immune checkpoint inhibition with anti-angiogenesis for enhanced efficacy in oncology.
  • MoAs: MK-1308A is a bispecific antibody that blocks PD-1 to enhance T-cell-mediated immune response and inhibits VEGF to reduce tumor angiogenesis.
  • Sources: Fierce Biotech, BioSpace

item.title: Revolution Medicines (Daraxonrasib/RMC-6236)
item.description:
• Summary: Revolution Medicines reported positive data for RMC-6236 (daraxonrasib), a pan-RAS inhibitor, in pancreatic cancer, leading to a stock surge and buyout speculation. The data underscore the potential of targeting RAS mutations, a common driver in cancers.
  • MoAs: RMC-6236 is a small molecule inhibitor that covalently binds to mutant KRAS proteins, blocking downstream signaling pathways involved in cell proliferation and survival.
  • Sources: Endpoints News, Endpoints News

item.title: Replimune FDA Dispute
item.description:
• Summary: Replimune is challenging the FDA over regulatory flexibility for its oncolytic virus therapies, arguing for tailored approval pathways. This dispute occurs amid public debate on FDA stringency, impacting the development of cancer immunotherapies like RP1.
  • MoAs: Replimune's oncolytic viruses (e.g., RP1): Engineered to selectively replicate in and lyse cancer cells while stimulating anti-tumor immune responses.
  • Sources: Biospace
